videos.title: 马斯克又放大招！特斯拉发生3大变动，股价又要跌了？
videos.transcript: Hello everyone, I'm Mingtaojun, your stock explorer. Recently, Tesla has done something that shocked everyone. It has issued a financial report that is incomprehensible. It is this financial report that has triggered a fierce debate in Wall Street. And the stock price has also increased by 5% after the financial report, and then dropped by 5%. At the end of the night, Tesla showed extremely contradictory financial data. And in the development of technology, the hard-to-reach autonomous driving and robot taxis have all changed. And most importantly, Musk is starting to play big again. This time he wants to build a chip. For this, he has invested all his cash flow to build a system. It seems that this Tesla financial report is extremely important. Its importance is definitely not reflected in the stock price of the past two days. And I found that almost all the analysis on the market only remained on the surface. So in this video, MetaTorch decided to give you a deep analysis from a professional perspective of Tesla's latest financial reports. We dig deep into financial data and Tesla's business research, hoping to bring you a deeper decision basis. At the end of the video, I will give my personal clear stock price prediction and investment point of view. Before the video starts, MetoJun would like to ask you to help me with a small favor. If you have time, please help me click the link in the comment section to "Growth Securities IB" No need to register, no need to open a account, just click the link alone, which is the biggest support to MetoJun. The growth of the Securities IB is also one of the easy-to-use terms for me. One of the main characteristics of IB is that the cost is low. It is not only the bottom of the transaction fee, but also the cost of financing is very low. If you are a deep trader, the function of the IB trading platform is also very powerful, especially the full transaction function, which is easy to use. They now provide extremely high risk-free interest benefits. The maximum interest for investment in your investment account can be obtained by 3.14%. In addition, they also provide various free research reports, trading tools and timely news updates. All these functions are to help ordinary investors get the most comprehensive help in trading. In fact, two weeks before the financial report, Tesla's latest car and energy sales have been announced. The result is a great surprise. The car business has only paid 35.8 million in the first quarter, which is far below the market expectation of 37.1 million. And the energy business, which has been stable, has also fallen this time. But only two weeks later, the latest financial report data is unusually bright. The stock price has also risen by 5% after the market was closed. So why is that? Let's dig deeper into the financial report data together. The following picture is the income performance of Tesla. The latest quarter's income is $22.4 billion, which is 16% of the total money growth, which is basically in line with the market expectations. From the picture, it can be seen that this growth looks good In fact, it was caused by the low technology last year In fact, the car delivery and energy business of this quarter have just said that it is not ideal The reason is because of Trump's "Great American Law" The law stipulates that at the end of September 2025, all electric cars and energy storage businesses will be cancelled This led to a lot of early sales in the third quarter of last year It also shows the sales performance of the next two quarters But although the income performance is average Tesla gave a bright performance in terms of profits The following picture shows Tesla's interest rate interest rate, especially the interest rate of car sales is the most important indicator for Tesla It directly reflects Tesla's competitive industry, pricing power and its productivity efficiency The old audience should know that this data is also the key decision-making basis for our many accurate predictions of Tesla's stock price This quarter, Tesla's overall interest rate reached 21.1% and exceeded the expected value The interest rate of car sales after deducting points increased to 19.2% which is 16.6% higher than the market expected 17.9% higher than last quarter 1.3% higher The reason for the significant increase in the interest rate is partly due to the increase in income from the tax and tax returns The other part comes from the increase in the ratio of Model S and Model X, which are high-cost cars The sales ratio of these two high-end cars increased from 2.8% last quarter to 4.5% this quarter The reason is simple because they are going to stop production So they forced the local people to buy them at a high price But these two reasons are one-off It's hard to repeat in the future The real important thing is the active contribution of FSD subscription We will analyze this in detail later Let's first analyze the performance of net profit From the point of view of cost Due to the increase in AI investment and the increase in equity opportunities Tesla's cost this quarter has increased by 42% significantly But despite this Tesla still achieved a significant increase of net profit of 56% Reached $14.5 billion $12.4 billion, much higher than expected And this still falls under the company's bright-eyed interest rate performance In general, the financial data of this time is not so bad. Although the income is not good in the first quarter, this is mainly because the US-US trade agreement has passed through the sales after the sale. Now half a year has passed, the effect of this transfer has been almost exhausted. On the other hand, the Iranian war pushed the oil price up. It is estimated that it will be difficult to fully recover in the short term. This will also stimulate the sales of electric vehicles to a certain extent. So I expect that the future sales of electric vehicles will be greatly affected. In terms of profits, the company's performance is very good Of course, there is a part of this is a one-off improvement But the most critical FSD performance is really good This is also the most concerned about the market The biggest factor that affects the stock price Next, let's focus on the analysis of the FSD situation This picture is my summary of Tesla FSD subscription performance The first quarter FSD subscription amount reached 1.28 million taels The share price increased by 16.4% The share price increased by 51% From the picture, we can see that the subscription of FSD in the first quarter has a clear proposal. The increase of 180,000 in the quarter is already higher than the growth of the whole year in 2025. FSD data was just revealed last quarter. At that time, I predicted that the growth of FSD would lead to breakthroughs, otherwise the company would not be willing to disclose this data alone. Now it seems to be verified. So how does this 1.28 million subscription actually penetrate? Tesla didn't disclose this data alone I went to calculate it myself and I was still shocked Because FSD is only open to use in North America now So we just calculate the situation in North America With the total amount of FSD subscriptions, the total sales of Tesla accumulated in the United States and Canada can get such a rough penetration rate This number is actually 38% high Of course, this number may be a bit high Because of the 1.28 million subscribers, some of them are free for new users. If we throw this part out, the penetration rate will drop from 30% to 35%, but this is enough to surprise. It can be seen that FSD has now begun to run. This is the result of FSD. How will it develop in the future? The focus is on two points. One is to review, the other is to model and tie. In April, Tesla just got the FSD approval in the Netherlands This is equivalent to opening the entry channel of the European market It is expected that the main EU countries' reports will be completed by the end of this year There will be more European countries approved FSD in the future And China has also received some approval It is expected that the third quarter will see a substantial progress Considering China's supervision restrictions and the low payout of users FSD penetration in China is not the focus in the short term The focus is on Europe Tesla's storage car size in Europe is not small, plus the European users themselves have a high cost-benefit ratio. Once FSD is approved, with a penetration rate of nearly 40% in the United States, Europe has a huge room for growth. On the model belt, Musk gave a specific plan at the financial meeting. The FSD V15 version is expected to be launched at the end of this year or early next year. The V15 version will be fully based on AI operation, and safety will be significantly improved, even beyond the level of humans. At the same time, Musk also revealed that FSD will gradually start to release unmanned surveillance to customers and cars in the fourth quarter. It can be seen that the V15 version will be an important decisive factor in determining the five-person surveillance of FSD. Don't look at the income ratio of FSD now, it seems not very high, every quarter is 3-4 billion dollars, less than 2%, but it has a structural impact on Tesla's overall valuation. Because FSD has a very high profit rate and it is a subscription mode, low assets and high growth It is a completely different business model from selling cars Its commercial value is higher I follow Tesla in Metal Pro Every quarter will update Tesla's valuation model In the last quarter's valuation model, we calculated that the value of FSD's individual business is between $53 to $86 The valuation ratio is 15% to 20% The specific valuation calculation is not here, the emotional judge can check it in Metal Pro In addition, according to the latest financial report, the complete Tesla valuation model will also be launched by Metapro next week. I will make a comprehensive valuation of all businesses such as Tesla's electric cars, FSD, Robotaxi, energy, etc. Everyone is looking forward to it. Now we can see that FSD is very important for Tesla's stock price, and its development is very positive now. The sales and penetration rate of the first quarter have been greatly improved, and in the future, with the continuous approval of Europe and China, the income potential is also very high, and it can continue to bring increases. And by the end of the year, the release of V15 may bring no one to monitor FSD This will be another start to the growth of subscription So to say that this quarter is the turning point for FSD growth I think it's not a big deal You may ask Since Tesla's financial data and FSD's progress are so active Then why did Tesla's stock price finally fall? This is going to be the other two negative concerns They are all related to Musk The first big concern is that Musk is used to jumping tickets again. First of all, it's the jump ticket of Robotaxi. The progress of Robotaxi was not bad. Just before the release of this week's financial news, Tesla quietly launched a robot taxi with no driver in Dallas and Houston. Although there are only two cars, the direct version is a version without a driver. It's still faster than the market expected. According to the plan, it should be launched in five regions this year. There are a total of 612 cars in the four areas that have been listed, of which 512 cars are equipped with safety equipment. Most of Austin's teams are also equipped with safety equipment. There are less than 20 cars that are not driven by anyone. You can see that since the robot taxi was launched in June 2025, the expansion speed of the team has been relatively slow. But at least in the city space, it is still a step ahead. But at this press conference, Musk made a jump on the progress bar again. This time, the robot taxi is to contribute financial income. The original plan was to be delayed to 2027 in the second half of this year. In fact, whether it is the slower car fleet expansion speed or the delay of this time, there is only one core reason. That is safety. Musk is not satisfied with the security of the current FSD V14.3 version. According to the analysis of the FSD above, the expected structural change in safety will be the launch of the V15 version. Because the launch of V15 will also wait until the end of the year or next year, so the change of Robotaxi is likely to take longer. Another jump event is the Optimus robot. Originally, V3 models were supposed to be two in the first quarter, but now it has been delayed to three quarters. The official reason is that they are worried that competitors will overrun, so they are more inclined to release it when it is close to mass production. However, the production line and mass production time table are still maintained for the second half of the year, which has not changed. We know that Tesla's stock price is only a small part of the basic support, and most of it is going to rely on these future technology trends. Musk's habitual "jump" obviously brings a higher uncertainty to the company's short-term stock price prospects. But the real uncertainty is not from the "jump", but from Musk's other move: making chips. Musk said at a financial meeting that in order to set up the Terafab chip factory, they will increase capital investment from the $2 billion expected to $2.5 billion. You know, the 2 billion was already the capital expenditure that Musk mentioned at the end of last year. In the previous few years, Tesla's annual capital investment was less than 1 billion. So from this year on, the progress of the chip factory and the increased capital spending will become a new variable that will disturb Tesla's stock price. So how much does this capital spending affect Tesla? We use data to talk. This is the current performance of Tesla's free cash flow. Tesla's current capital spending is around 20-25 billion per quarter, and the free cash flow that deducts these capital spending is relatively positive. That is to say, Tesla's total capital spending of about 1 billion a year is very abundant and very safe, and the capital spending has been increased from 1 billion to 250 billion, which is completely different. The following picture is a prediction of Tesla's free cash flow It is expected that free cash flow will turn around this year And it's hard to turn around in 2027 The last time this kind of negative cash flow occurred It will be traced back to 2017 Tesla was even threatened with bankruptcy at that time Therefore, this large-scale investment also greatly increased Wall Street's concern about Tesla Is this concern reasonable? Objectively speaking Tesla's current situation is completely different from 2017 The negative cash flow of 2017 is that they really have no money And now Tesla's cash and cash add-ons add up to $44.7 billion, and they can make $1.5 billion in every quarter Even after the expansion of capital, they can't afford it at all It's hard to see from the data that this negative cash flow will not continue to last. It can be completely turned over in one to two years So the problem with this kind of capital spending is not about financial security, but what it's invested in Tesla's CFO said at the conference that the capital-based spending mainly focused on TerraFab AI computing and several new factories such as CyberCamp, Optimus, and battery. The core of this is the TerraFab AI computing factory. In fact, we are not unfamiliar with this narrative. Almost all big technologies are actively investing in AI computing. And the current Wall Street is a huge-scale AI investment. There is no difference in punishment. From a short-term perspective, this kind of punishment makes sense. Because Tesla's chip manufacturing is destined to bring a higher uncertainty to the company. A new business, whether its progress is smooth, whether it can be modified, when it can be modified, these are all new unknowns. The increase in uncertainty should naturally reflect the short-term price. But if we look at the long term, we will find that this kind of investment is inevitable and has a strategic meaning. Tesla's FSD, Robotaxi, Optimus, humanoid robots are all AI applications in the physical world. Once commercialization is in progress, the demand for chips will be sky-high If from design to manufacturing to supply chain all rely on external suppliers, it will inevitably be restricted to people This is why Apple also claims that chips are the reason This is almost an inevitable option for all physical AI to run And Tesla's Terra5 chip factory is to solve this problem It includes the entire system from chip design to chip manufacturing to seal testing and supply chain insurance In fact, Tesla claims that chips are not just starting but have already been deployed Just a week ago, Tesla's self-proclaimed AI5 chip was successfully leaked, which indicates that Tesla is really starting to master the core of AI in the physical AI path. Admit that these layout is still difficult to see a clear return, but these are the basic facilities that will be indispensable for future physical AI. So do you think such a large amount of capital investment is necessary to worry about? I think financial security is basically not to worry about. But the uncertainty of short-term growth is indeed objective And such investment is also difficult to see a return in the short term According to the above analysis Robotex's big-sale transformation was delayed to 27 years It can be seen that the return has been delayed but the investment has increased This will naturally cause market concerns The stock price will also be suppressed But once you have a long career, you will find that this concern will eventually be released And it doesn't affect the company's long-term investment logic Meta is also an old Tesla shareholder. Based on my experience in the past few years, I summarized this set of Tesla's four-prong investment logic. I think the old members of MetaPro should have heard this wrong. In order to take care of our new audience, I will explain again. Now, there are two lines to decide the price of Tesla. One is its performance basics. This mainly refers to its sales business. The other is its technology narrative. This mainly includes the progress of FSD, Robotaxi, and Optimus human-like robots. According to the strong and weak progress of these two lines Tesla's stock price has four corresponding rise and fall rates According to this framework I expect Tesla in the next few months The probability will be in the low trend of technology demand The good basic side of this trend Why do you say that I'll give you a look at Tesla's current situation On the positive side The basic side of Tesla should not be too bad in the next half of the year after the stop of the large beauty supplement The sales should be reduced in the future Plus the background of high oil prices Car and energy business The probability will be good In addition, FSD growth is also very promising. The 40% penetration rate of North America is a sample of European penetration. As the European region gradually opens up, there will be a good growth momentum in the future. And I think there is also a further room for growth in the North American itself. From my personal experience of FSD, I can't live without FSD anymore. My FSD value ratio has increased from 30% in the beginning to 70% now. Tesla owners in front of the screen, why don't you share your experience with us? On the negative side, the uncertainty of the increase in capital-based spending has been brought about by the increase in capital-based spending. And the technology-related Li Chengbei incident has been delayed. This includes the FSD V15, which has been delayed from this year's third quarter to the end of the year, and even next year. Robotech's fleet, large-scale expansion and financial contributions have also been delayed to 2027. It can be seen that investment is constantly increasing, but transformation is constantly delaying. This will significantly weaken the price of moving up in the short term. This is why it is said that Tesla will be in the short term. The reason for the trend of the weak basic surface is to know that Tesla's stock price is not simply supported by the basic surface. So the good basic surface alone is not enough to provide enough growth. And in this lack of basic surface support, the market trend is relatively weak. The risk of the stock price is naturally higher and the fluctuation is greater. Therefore, I personally believe that the stock price of short-term Tesla is not enough to rise, and the risk will be relatively high. But if you are looking at the performance of medium-long-term Tesla, then I think Tesla's investment logic has not changed at all, but has become stronger. I believe that Tesla's three technology trends will eventually come to an end, and we don't need to wait too long. It's just that this financial report has just slightly delayed the time. For long-term investors, the delay in the line-up is not terrible. As long as the long-term investment logic remains unchanged, it is still a good company with a high investment value. Looking back at Tesla's stock price in the past 10 years, the big breakthrough of each stock price is a major event behind it, changing its stubborn logic. The wave of 2020 drives the stock price is the production and release of Shanghai factories. Tesla is a company that has no profits and is approaching bankruptcy, to the profit-reference, the company that has the potential to sell cars. By 2024, the three-generation technology trend will no longer let the market use the car-based structure to solidify Tesla, but will completely adopt the solidified model of technology companies. However, this is the stage when Tesla's technology trend is back to the stage when it was selling cars without making money. And the next breakthrough in the future, I think, will definitely be the progress of the three-generation technology trend, from the solidification of dream to the solidification of profit. And this node is expected to be realized at the early stage of this year. and the breakthrough of the stock price is likely to come earlier. At this time, it can be said that the physical AI trend has reached the stage of profit-making before the dawn of profit. Whether it is the industry space or the stock price space, there is a huge potential for growth. So even if you wait for more than three months or half a year, from the perspective of future revenue space, I think it is also worth it. And when it comes to investment operations, if I have hidden Tesla, then I think the key in the short term will be risk control. Don't be eliminated before the end of the day. But if I look at the future of Tesla's technology trend, there is no such stock, then the short-term risk will be called a good buy-in opportunity. Finally, I would like to give you a few potential risks. The first is the red-line level. If oil prices continue to operate at a high level, then the market will always exist for the exchange rate of the price. For companies like Tesla, which has a high valuation and needs to expand capital-based spending in the future, this red-line environment is relatively unfriendly. This means that the fluctuation of the stock price will be further magnified. The second risk is related to another company of Tesla SpaceX SpaceX is expected to IPO in July this year The current market value for it is about 180 million It has surpassed Tesla There is now such a rumor in the market SpaceX is likely to absorb Tesla This possibility does exist After all, Musk has limited energy If you put all your business under one company The efficiency of management will be higher And from the business logic, XAI and rocket launch businesses under Tesla's electric car, FSD, humanoid robot and SpaceX can indeed be opened into a set of complete AI + physics world closed ring. Some people may be curious, if SpaceX really wants to acquire Tesla, the purchase price will definitely be a hundred million. Isn't this a good thing for shareholders? Why is it a risk? This is related to the unique trading structure of Tesla's stock. Tesla's stock has a large number of "Libcals" Some of them are long-term "cals" over $600 Once there is a certain price of purchase These long-term "cals" will completely lose their meaning The amount of transaction will be greatly reduced In the current stock structure of Tesla These "Libcals" with bars have a large transaction ratio Once this part of the bar fund is withdrawn Even if the stock transaction volume increases Overall, there will still be a certain pressure on the stock price This is also one of the reasons why Tesla's stock price has fallen recently Of course, how much pressure is there? To be honest, I can't exactly measure it But for those investors who have Tesla stock in the medium to long term, this matter is not to worry at all Even if SpaceX really acquires Tesla in the end, the purchase price will be the same price For those stockholders, it is still a real good thing Okay, I have analyzed all the latest Tesla financial reports with you If you like Meta's analysis, please subscribe to this channel. We analyze deep into the US stock every week. But usually, I share a lot of community posts every day, and share some short and fast investment opportunities and investment opinions. Only by subscribing to the channel can you not miss these key investment information. If you want to know more about stock research, you are welcome to come to my MetaPro. MetaPro is a professional stock research service that I created for ordinary investors. In the MetaPro video updated next Monday, I will analyze such a cold-door company for you. It has a good potential for growth in the AI era But unlike traditional AI stocks It also has a good defense attribute The more the market fluctuates, the more it benefits If you recognize the big trend of AI outbreak But worry about the wrong direction Then this company is worth your attention And because the company is very old Almost no one in the market can analyze it We can analyze it because each team member has a related work experience I was lucky enough to find out and understand the investment logic of this company Thanks for watching, welcome to subscribe to Metopro New users are free to use in the first few days Come and experience it If you like the video of Metojun Don't forget to like, follow and forward Thank you all

videos.title: 段永平为何67亿抄底泡泡玛特？能抄作业吗？| 拉布布 | Labubu | popmart | 盲盒 | 情绪价值
videos.transcript: 最近有一个段子特别的火 从段永平最近雪球的发言来看 又是注射替尔伯泰 又是每天有氧40-60分钟 还突然看懂了 泡泡玛特提供的情绪价值 对此合理的推断是 他找了一个年轻20岁的女朋友 玩笑归玩笑 （最近一次财报）这家公司利润暴涨了接近300% 但是股价却在两天跌了30% 两年前段永平还说看不懂这家公司 绝不会投资 但是3月底 却宣布收回之前不投资的言论 一边是市场恐慌抛售 另一边是大佬逆势布局 到底是市场错了 还是段永平变了 段永平为什么会出现态度反转 他的卖put操作 究竟又有什么样的玄机 今天这期视频 我们跟着段永平的认知变化走 从“看不懂”到“重仓” 从股价暴跌到期权操作 一步步拆解 段永平投资泡泡玛特的投资逻辑 hello大家好 这里是美股博士 每周分享美股干货 片尾有我的实盘 千万记得看到最后 今天这期视频分成三个部分 第一部分 泡泡玛特这家公司最近怎么了 第二部分段永平对泡泡玛特 这家公司的认知是如何变化的 第三部分 段永平是如何通过卖put挣钱的 首先第一部分 先跟大家快速的理清 泡泡玛特究竟怎么了 先说市场为什么在卖 泡泡玛特的2025年年报一发布 直接交出了王炸业绩 营收371.2亿元 同比增长了184.7% 净利润130亿元 同比暴涨了284.5% 放在任何行业都是顶级的增长数据 但是诡异的是 年报发布当天 股价直接暴跌22.5% 两天累计跌了30% 市值蒸发了600亿港元 市场的恐慌主要来源于两个担心 第一就是单IP依赖的风险 泡泡玛特当前的核心是拉布布 单IP年收入占比接近四成 市场的担心就是 一个IP撑不起长期增长 万一没有后续的爆款IP 增长就会乏力 第二就是增长放缓的焦虑 泡泡玛特的CEO王宁在年报里明确说 2026年公司的增长目标是不低于20% 对比2025年180%的爆发式增速 市场直接解读为增速见顶 觉得这家公司的黄金增长期 已经过去了 所以很多投资者选择用脚投票 直接把股价砸到了160港元附近 就在市场恐慌抛售的时候 4月9号 段永平在雪球发言 "我的泡泡玛特保险公司开张了" 熟悉段永平的朋友都知道 他这句话意味着什么 段永平选择卖put的方式 开始准备建仓泡泡玛特了 但是从先前段永平的发言记录来看 他之前评价泡泡玛特是"看不懂" 为什么他现在又突然看懂了 期间到底发生了什么 接下来我们就进入第二部分 段永平对于泡泡玛特的认知变化之路 结合段永平在雪球上的发言 我把他对这家公司的认知变化 概括为三个阶段 第一阶段就是2026(口误)年之前 段永平早期明确表示 无法理解人们为什么需要盲盒 他不理解的原因在于 盲盒背后的情绪消费到底是一时潮流 还是永续需求 他当时觉得 年轻人买盲盒就是跟风炒作 就像曾经的集卡网红玩具一样 热度褪去 需求就会消失 而且他认为 泡泡玛特的生意太依赖年轻人的审美 审美变化太快 风险太高 所以他坚守不懂不做的原则 明确表示自己不会投资 但是在2026年3月 他的认知迎来了变化 3月30号 段永平表示 他收回先前 关于不投资泡泡玛特的言论 让段永平态度动摇的 我根据段永平的发言记录 揣测归纳出了三点原因 首先就是年报数据的硬实力 2025年的业绩暴涨 它不是偶然 而是持续增长的体现 因为这家公司高毛利高现金流低负债 泡泡玛特的毛利率高达72.1% 净利润35.2% 这个水平堪比茅台 卖100块钱的产品 就能产生35块的净利润 而且 这家公司还不需要大量重资产投入 生产可以外包 核心成本是设计和IP授权 回款速度快 2025年的经营现金流达到了108.65亿元 资产负债率不到30% 财务状况极其稳健 这种轻资产高盈利的模式 抗风险能力极强 大概率是段永平最看重的稳 第二就是实地考察真实需求 在欧洲的门店里 外国成年人主动为中国IP买单的场景 打破了他 “盲盒是中国年轻人专属”的固有认知 让他意识到这种情绪需求是全球化的 这门生意的长期潜力远超他的预期 年轻人买盲盒 买的是拆盒的惊喜 IP的收藏感 更是缓解生活压力的治愈感 这种情绪消费很有可能是长期的 和手机白酒不同 情绪消费的稳定性 更依赖于IP的持续运营能力 这也是风险所在 而泡泡玛特的优势 在于它的IP工业化能力 能够持续孵化运营 守住情绪消费的基本盘 这款茅台的社交属性苹果的体验生态 理论上 都是能够穿越周期的核心竞争力 目前 泡泡玛特的业务覆盖了20多个国家 海外收入占比达到了43.8% 但对比乐高迪士尼的全球渗透率 它的海外布局才刚刚起步 长期增长空间巨大 第三就是王宁的长期主义 他看了王宁的访谈 看到王宁主动降低增速目标 专注IP孵化 不贪短期快钱不盲目扩张的“本分” 这种企业家的特质 和段永平自己的投资哲学高度契合 让他觉得这家公司的创始人是“对的人” 第三个阶段 就是今年4月的重仓布局阶段 一般价值投资者认为 好公司好团队好价值三者缺一不可 好公司和好团队 我在上一部分已经介绍完了 最重要的就是好价格 股价暴跌之后 这家公司的估值 从40倍PE砸到了14倍左右 这里要重点说一句 估值下降的本质 是市场对于未来增长的重新定价 重点不是14倍的PE是不是便宜 而是这个估值 能不能够匹配它未来的增长质量 和确定性 如果泡泡玛特能够实现20%的增长 那么14倍的估值并不算贵 如果他守不住20%的增速 那么当前14倍的估值就算不上是便宜 从段永平的买入操作 我们可以合理推断 他认为 这家公司长期增长的确定性足够 14倍PE算得上 是已经具备安全边际的便宜价格 更关键的是 段永平看到了 市场恐惧和公司价值之间的差距 这就是价值投资的核心 别人恐惧我贪婪 聊完了段永平认知变化的过程 接下来我们再看他的操作 他并没有选择 直接购买泡泡玛特的股票 而是选择了卖put的方式 用期权工具实现了风险可控 接下来我们就用最通俗的语言 跟大家拆解 段永平是如何通过卖put的方式 为买入泡泡玛特这家公司的股票 做准备的 首先段永平卖put 他并不是炒期权赚快钱 而是为了用更便宜的价格 买入泡泡玛特 核心是被动建仓 风险可控 而不是稳赚不赔 段永平自己说 卖put就是开保险公司 这里我们不用记复杂的金融术语 我跟大家拆解成大白话 对于买方来说 他担心泡泡玛特的股价下跌 花了一笔权益金向卖方买一个承诺 到期日如果股价低于约定价 他有权把股票以行权价卖给卖方 而卖方段永平收了买方的权利金 承诺到期之日如果股价低于行权价 就必须以行权价买入约定数量的股票 简单说段永平就是收了一笔保费 要么白赚保费 要么以更低的价格买入股票 这是一种收益结构不对称的操作 风险可控 但是并不是没有风险 段平具体怎么操作的 我跟大家一步步拆解一下 第一步 就是先确定行权价 泡泡玛特暴跌之后 在160港币附近 段平认为它的合理价格在200港币以上 当然由于段永平并没有披露 每一笔期权的行权价和权利金 为了方便大家理解 我只能用一个简单的虚构的数字模型 来跟大家说明这种策略的运作方式 假设他选择了180港币作为行权价 这个价格比市场价略高 但是在段永平看来已经足够便宜 即使跌到这个价 他也愿意长期持有 他在期权账户卖出了1,000万股 新权价180港币 一个月后到期的put 假设权利金是5港币 那么阿段立刻就可以收到5,000万港币的权利金 但需要注意的是 这笔权利金并不是完全的没有约束收益 账户仍需按照规则占用保证金 或者准备足额现金 用于潜在的行权义务 在一个月之后 有两种情况 第一股价高于180港币 这个时候期权通常会失效 买方不会行权 相当于在等待期间 获得了5,000万港币的额外收益 情况b就是股价低于180港币 买方这时候会行权 段永平则需要以180港币的价格 买入股票 由于之前已经收取了5港币的权利金 因此它的实际成本就是175港币 相当于在约定的买入基础上 获得了一定的折扣 但是 这个价格不一定低于当时的市场价格 整体来看 这种策略在看对公司 并采用现金备兑的前提下 具有等待也有收益 下跌则低价建仓的特点 但本质上仍然承担了股价下跌的风险 有些人可能觉得这个操作太香了 但是段永平也多次强调了 对于不懂的公司 千万不要随意的卖put 否则会出现血本无归 卖put需要两个前提 二者缺一不可 前提一就是自己要足够懂这家公司 只卖自己看得懂 而且愿意长期持有的公司 比如段永平自己眼里的苹果茅台 泡泡玛特等等 这些公司基本面扎实 经营状况良好 利润丰厚 就算被套牢也能长期拿 第二就是现金备兑 账户里要存有行权所需的全部现金 不要加杠杆 防止在股价暴跌的时候被强行平仓 这是风险控制的核心 另外还要警惕四类风险 第一类风险就是踏空 如果股价一直上涨 你可能只是赚了一些权益金 但是 可能永远会买不到这家公司的股票 这是长期投资者需要承担的潜在风险 除此之外 还有极端下跌的风险 比如公司的基本面变化 流动性风险 比如期权无法平仓 以及股价波动带来的心理压力 总结一下 卖put它并不总是投机工具 而是段永平被动建仓 赚取等待收益的方式 这里的核心并不是期权的技巧 而是他对公司定价权的延伸 是他对于好价格投资理念的延伸 真正的高手 他并不会赌明天股价是涨是跌 而是在风险可控的范围内 赚自己认知范围以内的钱 当然这个认知的范围 也可以通过自己后天学习去积极拓展 讲到这儿 肯定会有人反驳 泡泡玛特靠的就是一个IP来反复炒作 本质上就是击鼓传花 热潮褪去 拉布布一文不值 甚至有人说 拉布布跟先前的网红玩具 其实没有区别 只是换了一个包装 其实这种质疑我认为是非常合理的 我们也不需要去回避 因为IP行业 它本身就存在生命周期的风险 审美会变 潮流会退 这也是泡泡玛特这家公司 股价剧烈波动的本质 分歧的本质 它并不是谁对谁错 而是大家对于IP的生命周期 究竟有多久 存在不同的意见 再回到片头的问题 是市场错了 还是段永平错了 其实都不是 市场看的是短期的增速 而段永平想要的是长期回报 市场被情绪左右 而段永平用平常心看待 坚持了他自己的投资逻辑 在我整理 段永平对于泡泡玛特资料的时候 他的部分观点我是认同的 也有一部分观点我是不认同的 他去年12月说的一句让我印象深刻 “人生很忙 看不懂的东西可以skip” 我对于泡泡玛特旗下的产品 并不是那么看好 因为我觉得 这类产品 能够成为下一个迪士尼的概率太低了 所以我不会抄段永平的作业 买入泡泡玛特的股票 当然我必须要承认 我没法预测这家公司股价未来会怎样 但是投资的最大风险从来都不是波动 而是以为自己看懂了 从段永平的历史记录来看 他赚的 从来都是比别人更晚犯错能力的钱 所以我在这里希望 段永平能够从泡泡玛特上面挣到钱 如果你和段永平一样 有泡泡玛特的股票 非常欢迎你在评论区 跟大家交流你的买入逻辑 如果你和我一样没有泡泡玛特的股票 也欢迎你在评论区 分享你对这家公司的看法 好了 这就是关于段永平投资泡泡玛特 我想说的 接下来我们进入实盘公布 hello大家好 本周我的回报率是-2.32% 2022年3月以来账户回报率是147.54% 仓位方面没有调整 只有每周定投的微软 本周LLY公布了财报 2026年Q1总收入是198亿美金 同比增长了56% 净利润是74亿美金 同比暴涨了168% 这是LLY连续多个季度 交出超预期答卷之后 再次让市场感到意外 GLP-1双雄贡献了128亿美金的营收 降糖版一季度收入是86.62亿美金 减重版是41.6亿美金 两款产品贡献了总营收的65% 相较于2025年全年365亿美金的收入 今年一季度就贡献了全年的35% 这个增速放到任何一家成熟药企上 都堪称惊人 LLY的CEO戴瑞文在财报中评价 本季度开局强劲 美国市场的收入是121亿美金 同比增长了43% 美国以外的市场收入增长更快 达到了81% 特别是中国市场 中国区营收是6.93亿美金 同比增长了52% 增速位列各主要市场的前列 特别提及 降糖版的替尔泊泰 在中国被纳入医保目录 是海外销量增长的重要原因之一 同时LLY还有一款产品值得关注 就是他的Jaypirca 2026年Q1营收是1.65亿美金 而2025年Q4是1.48亿美金 这款药在一定程度上 可以看作是 百济神州王牌产品 Zanubrutinib的新一代版本 针对的是 包括Zanubrutinib在内的 一二代BTK抑制剂 耐药之后的患者 这款非共价的BTK抑制剂 是全球首个且唯一 一季度贡献了2.15亿美金的营收 算得上不错 如果26年的营收能够突破10亿美金 那么这款产品绝对算得上是一款成功的产品 比较值得关注 当然就像我先前节目里提到的那样 10亿美金对于其他规模的药企 算得上成功 但是对于当前LLY的体量 就没有那么突出了 毕竟LLY的GLP-1双雄 一天就能卖一个多亿美金 所以如果你关注了百济神州 那么这款产品的销量就很值得关注 但是如果你只关注LLY 那就不要过度拔高这款产品 在这家公司的地位 好了如果你已经看到这里 非常非常希望你能点一下置顶评论区 盈透证券的开户链接 只要简单的点一下 然后关闭 就可以从后台的数据看到 只有1/40的人点了我这个链接 您点击这个链接 对于支持这个频道长期更新非常重要 非常感谢 好了 这就是今天这期视频的全部内容 我们下期视频不见不散

从目前的走势来看 这里都有一个顶部的形态了 包括ARM都有一些顶部形态 但是这个顶部并不是压力非常大 所以这个位阶大家可以考虑进场 但是进场的思路不是说马上就买 一定要等小时级别走好 今天小时级别没有走好 目前AMD也没有走好 ARM也没有走好 但是从整体的趋势来看 没有问题 中长线还是可以拿着的 可以进场的 其实我认为 像特斯拉这段时间都没怎么涨 这里有一根表态的K线 然后在这个表态的K线附近 已经整理了 1234 已经整理了4天了 这个位阶是有支撑的 是有比较强的支撑 不仅有4月15号这根K线的支撑 现在也是严格的守在了月均线之上 如果想进场 像特斯拉这种形态的股票 这种位阶是可以进场的 无论是波段也好 短线也好 都可以考虑进场 因为如果跌破了4月15号这根K线 跌破了月均线 防守位就在这个附近 盈亏比非常的合适而 前期大涨的ARM也好 包括AMD也好 包括康宁也好 现在都是观察时期 而不是进场的时期 所以在明天FOMC会议决议出台之前 在鲍威尔最后一次演讲之前 在明天盘后 这几个权重公司财报公布之前 短线仓位我还是这个观点 短线仓位大家稍安勿躁 等方向出来以后再进场也来得及 也就是说 现在重点要关注自己手中的短线仓位 而从市场整体的走势来看 长线仓位波段仓位都没有任何的问题 最后祝大家明天交易顺利 我们下期视频再见

videos.title: 破解投資圈「窮人思維模式」
videos.transcript: 在我的YouTube留言區裡 有很多網友習慣尊稱我為老師 同時還存在另外一種聲音說 既然你投資這麼厲害 為什麼還要辛苦的 做視頻寫文章呢 這兩種說法 其實指向了同一個問題 就是說 在我們中文投資圈 缺乏一種成熟的 平等的 基於商業邏輯的認知體系 所以今天我就想來聊聊這個話題 為什麼我不希望你叫我老師 為什麼真正的職業投資者 反而需要做內容輸出 首先我們就先來說 老師這個稱謂 老師這個稱謂 在中文的語境裡頭 它是一個非常尊貴的詞彙 在2000多年的 這種儒家文化的影響之下 我們華人是有尊師重道的 這種傳統 那麼老師也有傳道授業解惑 這樣的一個神聖的職責 所以說 人們對老師這個稱謂 有著一種天然的敬畏感 那麼潛意識裡面 我們認為老師 是代表著掌握真理的那一方 但是這種文化慣性 如果帶入到金融市場裡面來 它就變成了一種 致命的認知缺陷 為什麼呢 因為金融市場 videos.title: 期权新手必看！6个超简单步骤快速上手 ｜ 期权实战教学
videos.transcript: HelloHello大家好欢迎来到我的期权频道 我们今天给大家带来一个主题零基础如果 说你没有任何的期权基础也能赚 我们通过六个超级简单的步骤 让很多的期权的新手和中手能够快速的上手期权并且赚钱 首先第一个步骤就是选择 一个期权策略 我们知道其实很多的期权的新手和中手不能稳健盈利 是因为在一开始接触期权的时候 听不同的ABC三个老师说了不同的策略导致 呢短时间接触过多的期权策略 你可能你的头寸布局了五个甚至十个不同的期权策略 put端可能有Ratio Spread call端呢可能有Bear Put Spread或者说是 Iron Condor等不同的策略导致整个 的投资组合比较的凌乱甚至不能出现一个比较 好的盈利曲线完全没有能激励 你继续学习期权 所以说我们提出的第一点就是大家一定要做到 少而精哪怕一招鲜比如说 我就会Sell Put 或者我只做Covered Call  都会比你同时的最开始在新手阶段上手很多策略要好 得多尤其在一开始阶段选择一个策略直到 你能够很熟练的掌握这一个策略再去扩展你的能力圈 那我们就来聊一下 如果说我们选择一个期权策略 那么选择哪个策略最适合 我这边提出了六个选项 每一个期权策略大家都可以翻看 我以前的视频这种标黄色的这种的标的视频 都是这种的干货硬核视频 大家都可以去分别的去看一下 我们分别来简单的聊 首先第一个我其实非常推荐最开始半年到1年半的新手玩家 利用Wheel Strategy来去做期权比如说 我们选择一个标普五百或者 BTC 这种的强势的标的不断的在低位去Sell Put 持有正股spot正股在高位不断的去shortCall 就通过Sell Put 加spot现货然后shortCall  这些简单的期权操作能够稳健的盈利 第二个策略 我会推荐呢叫bull put spread 什么是Bull Put Spread就是 我们short一个put在更低的行权价呢Long一个put 那么就是一个bull Put Spread 我们是一个权利金正收益类型的比如说 我们一直看牛标普五百或者说是BTC  比如说BTC 现在是108K 我在106Kshort了一个put 我在104Kbuy了一个put 不停的去做多看涨这个优质标的这是第二个策略 第三个策略就是bear Call spread 什么是Bear Call Spread 就是我们在高行权价short一个Call 我们在更高行权价再buy一个Call  我们整体是看不大涨 Bear Call Spread 我们看这个标的是走熊或者横盘不大涨 我们用bear Call spread 第四个策略呢我们就可以用Iron Condor这个策略 就是一个很经典的中性卖方这么一个铁鹰策略 我们通过short put然后呢buy put short Call 然后呢在高行权价呢buy Call 关于Iron Condor呢 我也出过之前的有一个叫10 Rules的 这么一个视频大家可以去翻看 第五个策略就是 Put Ratio Spread 我之前出了一个策略特别受大家欢迎说如果 说你只需要一个策略反复的去做 能够去养老的这么一个策略 那么就是 Put Ratio Spread 大家可以跳到这个视频 我会把和这几个相关的这个视频呢都放到评论区的链接里面 最后一个策略就是第六策略 我们就是看涨某一个标的当这个标的出现大跌的时候 我们short一个put再Long一个Call做一个看涨 这么一个Risk Reversal的策略 这是我们说第一步 那么第二步呢就是要根据 我们选定的这么一个期权策略 我们可能在这六个策略里面呢选择了一个 举例来讲我可能就选择了Wheel Strategy 那么呢我需要去怎么做呢 我需要去建立一下 我的观察列表我这边呢以crypto为例 可能我主要做的是BTC 然后呢BNB、Solana包括呢以太坊 这几个可能我把  它作为一个Watchlist 我去观察一下什么呢 它的ATM的这个implied volatility 那么比如我就建立了这么一个标的 我用Wheel Strategy 我就是一个强势的看涨我去做 如果说我用Bull Put Spread呢 其实也是强势看涨 比如说SPY或者说是Solana或者说以太坊 也是我刚才说的如果说用Bear Call Spread 我就是不是很强烈看涨 比如说我举例子我不是特别看好Trump这个标的 那我就不停的去short bear Call spread 但是要注意可能Altcoin呢目前的Spread 比较大你需要算好这种点差我是用short Call呢 还是用short Call Spread或者Bear Call Spread这种 策略去做最后一个呢就是Iron Condor Iron Condor呢不推荐这些比较点差不好的Altcoin 而相对来说推荐BTC 大家去做这种的Iron Condor的铁鹰这个策略 那么第三步呢 第三步我这边呢提出了一个特别简单的一个操作的方式 我这边呢以拼多多为例 我们只需要观察两个指标 大家不需要观察特别多的多的指标大家要知道其实有的时候 We did not have propellants or reactive things on the vehicle. And so the test site incurred very little damage and of course nobody was hurt in the incident. Yeah, as part of testing, this is loud, so you learn to not be super skittish as you're walking around. So, we have our booster here on site. We're going to be performing a cryop proof. So, it's a long one. >> Taking booster SM to cryo highfill proof. >> Cryoproof is the first time you actually put propellant onto the vehicle. This is a Raptor 3. V3 is a high performance boost engine. It's also designed to be reusable and highly reliable. We've learned a lot over the course of producing around 600 V2 engines. You can see the amount of simplification that's occurred between V2 and V3. The level of integration is really unique for rocket engine that comes from a lot of work among many engineers to consolidate the hardware. The effect of that is that it's actually a higher reliability design. So fewer parts is cheaper, faster to build. So that allows us to make a lighter vehicle, to make a more reliable vehicle to overhaul, just build a better rocket. Achieving full reusability of an engine like this is really something that people haven't done before. The goal is to get the engine to behave in a similar way to the engines on commercial airplanes. frequently in interactions with people outside of SpaceX. The perception is that things far beyond what have ever been done before are impossible. Things being impossible is really to be determined by breaking laws of physics. We are not breaking laws of physics. We're just trying to leverage them as effectively as we can. All right, attention all B19 operators. This is the final go now go poll for today's operations. Flight software >> go. >> Ay, >> go. >> Raptor >> go. >> Stage >> go. >> C >> go. >> GC2 >> go. >> GC1 >> go. >> FC1 >> go. Two guys. 10 9 8 7 6 5 4 3 2 Mission. >> Wow. We kicked out early. All teams triage alerts. Prepare for offload. >> Today we aborted our second attempt of the B19 full count static fire. We actually had another pad abort on the diverter. We made it through engine startup and then ultimately lost some of our sensors on one of the ramp manifolds. It basically said that the manifold pressure was lower than we believe that it probably was and it kicked us out at t plus 1.88 seconds. It's just tough cuz there are some things that are hard to test ahead of time. So the diverter you can test as much as you want, but you can't obviously test for what theoretical vibe and environment you're going to see when the engines are turned on. >> Anytime that you walk into an operation where you want to turn on 33 of these enormously powerful engines and get them all to work together and walk away from it in one piece, that is a win. I think every test is always a success and we had a bunch of findings on the ground systems, the vehicle and the engines that I think were really good to flag at this point. But definitely a bittersweet day. We have this saying called only the paranoid survive. The idea behind it is that there's an enormous amount of information and data that's coming to you. And if you can use that wisely, you can usually use that to figure out where things are going to go wrong in the future. And almost every time that we fail something or have something go arai, there were clues in previous data in previous operations that if you had known what to look for and have been good enough and paranoid enough to do so, could have found it there. It's been 10 months since Massiey's had the S36 anomaly. >> Ship 36 was a tough one. We had a CPV explode and it not only took out the rocket, but it took out a lot of the test site infrastructure over at Massy's. How do we go make sure if we have an anomaly again, the pad can take a beating and still survive? >> Losing a rocket is one thing. We've got a production line for rockets and we can go make more. Uh losing the physical infrastructure of the launch pad or the test site is very difficult cuz you need to go repair that over a period of many months before you can get back into testing. videos.title: 40歲生日，我決定不再”努力“了
videos.transcript: OK. Today I turned 40. Yeah, the day I'm recording this video is my lunar birthday. And on my 40th birthday, I made a decision. I'm going to stop hustling so hard. Now, I know that might sound like I've made enough money, and I'm ready to lie flat. Actually, it's the exact opposite. It's one of the hardest things I've ever done in my life. Because for the past 40 years, I realized the only thing I know how to do is be tense and push hard. But now I realize, if I want to unlock the next chapter of my life, I need something completely new, and that's relaxation. What? Relaxation? That's it? I know what you're thinking, but hear me out. Most people get relaxation completely wrong. Relaxing isn't lying flat, it's not just having fun, and it's definitely not a reward you only earn after you succeed. Instead, it's the prerequisite for breaking through your bottlenecks and opening the next door. And the reason I'm making it my life's focus at 40 comes down to three things: a point of confusion, a scar on my wrist, and a book. What's your name? Ray. Ray, I'm Ray. Thank you, thanks. So that confusion came from a quote by Naval. He said, if you want to get rich, spend your time learning, earning, or relaxing, outsource or ignore everything else. Learning and earning, I get that. I've been doing those two things for the last decade, and it's what I always preach on this channel and in my community. But why spend time relaxing? I could never really wrap my head around it systematically. Then, earlier this year, I randomly read a really old book called Psycho-Cybernetics, translated as Xin Li Kong Zhi Shu. After reading it, it cleared up my confusion about Naval's philosophy on relaxing. So today, I want to use my own experiences to talk about why, if we want to unlock life's next chapter, every one of us needs to learn how to relax. I'll just have a hot mocha, easy to sip. You guys always see me wearing this Apple Watch in my videos. Honestly, I don't really like wearing watches. I wear it mostly to cover up a massive scar on my left wrist. Hey, don't overthink it, I didn't cut myself or anything. But this scar, in a way, is a microcosm of my tense, wound-up life. This is my birthday dinner. When I was a freshman in high school, my mom opened a small restaurant outside the coal mine where we lived. It was a tiny business, but grueling work. Whenever I went home for the weekend, I'd go help out, often working late into the night, and then we'd push our tricycle back home. One day, I think the gas tank ran out or something, I forget the exact details. I just remember it was just me and my mom, and a bunch of customers were waiting, getting super impatient. So I took a kitchen knife to chop some wood and help start a fire. But I accidentally hacked right into my left wrist. Blood just started spraying everywhere. I could literally see the bone. I was terrified. But I didn't dare tell my mom, afraid I'd interrupt her work. I just told her I cut my hand by accident, grabbed my wrist, and ran to a small clinic. The doctor there stopped the bleeding and gave me a bunch of stitches. The clinic was super sketchy, and the doctor's skills were pretty limited. So when the wound healed, the nerves were messed up. To this day, the area around my thumb is totally numb. I can't feel a thing there, but if you touch the scar, it hurts like crazy and gets super tingly. So if anyone ever gets into a fight with me, all they have to do is hit my left wrist hard, and they can take me down instantly. When I got back to school with this injury and people asked what happened, I started lying. I said I was playing around with a kitchen knife and accidentally slashed myself while hacking at stuff. And this lie had a few different versions. Like sometimes, depending on who I was talking to, I'd say I got slashed in a fight when I was a kid. It made me sound tough. I told these lies so many times that eventually, I kind of believed them myself. It became my default answer whenever anyone asked. I didn't even realize I was lying anymore. So why did I lie? Originally, it was just because I didn't want people looking down on me. Other kids went on vacations during their time off, while I spent my holidays working for survival, helping out in a tiny, run-down restaurant, and hacking myself in a moment of panic. Later on, I just wanted to hide the scar and everything it represented deep down inside, to give myself a goal and the strength to change my destiny against all odds. So I just kept holding my breath, pushing through, trying to force my fate to change through sheer hustle. My sophomore year of high school, I worked insanely hard. I studied until 1 or 2 AM every night and woke up at 5. I shot up to third place in my science track, and kept my grades top-tier all the way through senior year. Everyone thought I was going to a top university. But I bombed the entrance exam and only got into a second-tier college. But then a magical thing happened when I went to this second-tier college. Without the pressure of the gaokao, without everyone's expectations and eyes on me, I actually started to let loose and be myself. I started reading, listening to English podcasts, even working part-time and starting mini-businesses. That period was the fastest time of growth and transformation in my entire life. In a way, it made me who I am today. OK, OK, I gotta stop here, or I could talk about myself all day. So why am I telling you all this? What does it have to do with relaxing? Well, after reading Psycho-Cybernetics, and looking back at this and many similar experiences in my life, I noticed a pattern with myself. Whenever I'm tense and wound up, I usually mess things up. To use a trendy phrase, the harder I try, the worse I fail. And it's only when I relax that good things actually happen. I've been saying this phrase a lot lately. It's "Good things only happen when you are relaxed." Why is that? In the book, the author talks about a success mechanism. In English, it's called the success mechanism. He says that built into every one of our subconscious minds is an auto-navigation system, just like a missile's guidance system. It automatically corrects your course and guides you toward your target. You don't need to tell it how to take every single step. You just set the target and let it run. It's basically what we mean when we say "manifesting your desires." There's a quote in The Alchemist that goes, "When you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you to achieve it." I'm sure most people have had experiences like this. But here's the catch: this system has one condition to operate. You have to be relaxed enough. When you're tense, anxious, and desperately trying to micromanage every single step, your conscious mind forces its way in and tries to hijack the auto-pilot. And the result is, you just jam the whole system up. It's like walking while carrying a full glass of water, trying not to spill it. The more you think, "Don't spill," and the harder you stare at the glass, the more you end up spilling. But if you ignore it, stop caring, and just relax, the water stays right in the cup. The book refers to this as the creative mechanism. It gets locked down by your forebrain. So the more you want it, the worse you perform. Specifically, your inability to relax holds you back in these four dimensions. We often think that pressure comes from motivation. I pushed myself to study hard to crush the gaokao, driven by this core belief. Counterintuitively though, that understanding might be completely wrong. The book mentions an experiment: when animals learn about a new environment without too much pressure, they form a very broad cognitive map, exploring all sorts of possibilities and gaining a comprehensive understanding of their surroundings. But if the pressure is super high, that cognitive map shrinks drastically. They only focus on the most urgent thing right in front of them, and go completely blind to everything else. "Pressure retards learning." Pressure directly slows down your learning. And it doesn't just make you slow; it makes your learning narrow. The higher the pressure, the more inefficient and narrow your learning becomes. This makes you inflexible. When you're finally facing a real challenge, your performance naturally drops. That explains why I messed up my college entrance exam. Because I didn't just fail from being too nervous and jamming my success mechanism. Fundamentally, learning under all that pressure made my scope way too narrow. I only knew how to take tests, and only specific kinds of tests. My whole way of thinking was completely rigid. So when faced with a real challenge like the Gaokao, I had zero flexibility and no ability to adapt. Looking at the bigger picture, this also explains a phenomenon a lot of people feel but can't quite put into words. Why is it that "small-town test machines" like me, who didn't come from a great background, did well in school and were great at taking tests, but once we enter society, we end up performing worse than kids who grew up in more relaxed environments, even if their grades weren't as good? We used to think it was just because they had more resources or better connections. But this book made me realize there's a deeper reason: we thought we were learning better, but actually, we were learning much narrower. They grew up in a more relaxed environment, so their cognitive map is way broader than ours. Meanwhile, those small-town test machines are much more likely to face high-pressure situations. So at the end of the day, "happy learning" actually makes sense. And our high-pressure background has been affecting our entire life trajectory. Now, this isn't to say that studying and working hard under massive pressure can't bring you success. But the secret is, if you want to unlock even greater success, you need to give yourself room to breathe and learn how to relax. I know what you're gonna say: "Easy for you to say! You can't just relax on command." "What if my circumstances don't allow me to relax?" "Rui, when you were a small-town test machine, you couldn't relax either, right?" "Now that you're well-off, it's easy for you to preach." Yeah, I don't deny that. For a lot of people in crisis mode, it's impossible to completely let go. But we have to understand that "relaxing" is actually a spectrum, not just a single state. And that brings me to my second point: your inability to relax is an overreaction. It turns you into a reactor in life, rather than an actor. The book says humans are fundamentally actors, not reactors. We aren't just ships drifting in the wind. We have goals and we have direction. If your reaction to your environment isn't pushing your goals forward, or is even holding you back, what you really should do is just not react. The worrying, the anxiety, and the stressing we do every day—that's exactly this kind of useless reaction. They don't help you move forward; they only keep you stuck. What you really need to do is stop reacting. For example, the book points out that the reason most of us worry, get anxious, and panic essentially stems from a really terrible mental habit: you always feel like you should be doing a million things right now. While you're working on Thing A, you suddenly remember Thing B isn't done, you want to do Thing C, and Thing D can't be delayed either. And the next passage from the author is something I absolutely love. He says that even on your busiest day, every single task can only come to you one by one. It has to. No matter how many problems, tasks, or pressures you have, they can only line up single file and come at you one at a time. That's just an objective law of reality. So when you have a million things swirling in your head, you're actually violating that reality. Because we overreact, we end up procrastinating, avoiding things, overthinking, worrying, or even getting crushed by the pressure. We fail to act as the one in control, deciding what to focus on in this exact moment, and that leads to us doing a terrible job at everything. That's why so many productivity books emphasize single-tasking and push back against multitasking. And when books teach us mindfulness and focusing on the present, they're essentially just following this exact same rule of taking things one step at a time. They're telling us to relax and stop overreacting. It's just that they usually focus more on the spiritual or wellness side without really explaining the underlying mechanics. But this book figured it all out and explained the logic perfectly over half a century ago. And this applies not just to a specific task, but to our entire life journey. We always say "life is a journey," but honestly, a lot of times we don't really grasp what that actually means. In recent years, a lot of books have been preaching "enjoy the process" and downplaying the importance of goals. Early this year on our channel, we read a book called Tiny Experiments, which even goes as far as saying you shouldn't set goals at all, you should set experiments. But that totally contradicts another popular narrative. There are endless "success" books and productivity guides teaching you how to set goals, make plans, and break down execution. So which one is actually right? Well, Psycho-Cybernetics gives a crystal clear answer: they're both right, you've just messed up the ratio. The book says humans are naturally goal-oriented animals. Without a goal, you wouldn't even start. So how could there even be a process? Therefore, goals are absolutely necessary. But once you've set that goal, you should actually be in "journey mode" for the vast majority of the time. In journey mode, you are an actor. You decide exactly where to step, and you even get to decide to enjoy the scenery along the way. But when you're only staring at the goal, you end up overreacting. Trying too hard turns you into a reactor, and you end up losing your footing. What you really should do is spend most of your time watching the road right under your feet, and only occasionally look up to make sure you're still heading the right way. Yet so many of us do the exact opposite. We spend too little time in journey mode and stare way too hard at the goal. The more you stare, the more the pressure pushes you from being an actor to a reactor, and you end up moving slower by trying to rush. The heavier your goals are, the more you need to learn how to relax. This is incredibly relevant to my life right now. I've mentioned before that I've achieved relative financial and time freedom, and now I only work on things I love, aiming for big goals. But I found that with such massive goals and so many projects, I'm constantly trying to solve every single problem in front of me as quickly as possible. It leaves me feeling suffocated. Sometimes I get so overwhelmed by the tasks— wanting to do everything right now but ending up doing nothing at all— that I forget to focus on the process and even want to just give up. And worse, it makes me beat myself up, thinking I'm just not capable enough. And that brings us to the next major way pressure ruins our progress. My 40th birthday is over. Let's go to the studio. Let's see if that security cat is still there. The other day on Twitter, I saw a highly debated clip of the billionaire Marc Andreessen. It sparked a ton of controversy. In an interview, he said he rarely self-reflects. He's even read over 400 biographies of great entrepreneurs, and found that almost none of them engage in self-reflection. "You don't have any levels of introspection." "Yes, zero. As little as possible." "Why?" "Move forward. Go. Yeah, I, I don't know." "I just find people who dwell on the past get stuck in the past. It's, it's just, it's a real problem and it's a, it's a problem at work and it's a problem at home." He criticized self-reflection as a modern invention, a psychological and philosophical movement driven by guilt. Great figures of the past didn't do this. They only looked outward and forward to build great things, instead of sitting around criticizing themselves and doubting themselves while living in the past. As soon as he said that, the internet blew up. His critics and his supporters started arguing fiercely, and Marc himself didn't let it go either— he kept getting into endless debates with his haters right there on Twitter. Now, I know his statement is definitely extreme, but after I watched it, I thought, doesn't this perfectly align with the point I've been trying to make? If we apply this idea of "self-reflection" to the success mechanism we've been talking about today, it's basically an error-correction mechanism. There's nothing inherently wrong with correcting errors. Your success mechanism needs it, just like a missile has to constantly adjust its trajectory to hit the target. But the book immediately follows up with a crucial prerequisite: for this error correction to actually work, it has to be subconscious, automatic and spontaneous. Meaning, the correction has to happen at a subconscious level. It has to be automatic and unprompted. Any conscious effort to engage in self-criticism and self- correction is fundamentally over-correcting, and it does more harm than good. Why? For example, if you're giving a speech on stage, your eyes will naturally scan the audience's faces. And if someone frowns, you might subconsciously slow down your pace, or explain things in a different way. You won't even realize you're doing it. That's course correction at the subconscious level. It runs quietly in the background, highly accurate, and very efficient. But if, during your speech, someone suddenly stands up and boos you, or the voice of self-criticism in your head gets loud enough, then this correction signal gets dragged from the subconscious right into your conscious mind. Your focus is completely shattered. You start doubting and second-guessing yourself, your system overloads, and you panic. The exact same signal helps you when it's subconscious, but becomes pure interference when it hits the conscious level. That's exactly how self-criticism works. Of course you need to correct mistakes and improve, but you should let your subconscious handle that process— auto-correcting and auto-adjusting. When you drag it into your conscious mind, turning it into "Why am I so bad?" or "I messed up again," or "Am I just a failure?"—then you're no longer correcting, you're attacking yourself. Your success mechanism isn't being fixed, it's being suppressed. So when we talk about relaxing, it really just means letting your conscious mind step back and letting your subconscious take the wheel. Being tense is just your conscious mind fighting for control. When your success mechanism is suppressed, the most obvious symptom is that you're afraid to be yourself. Why emphasize "being yourself"? Because deep down, every single one of us has a true self, and a natural instinct to express it. True success is finding and unleashing that authentic version of you, and answering that ultimate question: "Who am I?" But excessive self-criticism actively crushes that expression. I often say that as a YouTuber, succeeding is actually really simple: you just need to take how you express yourself off-camera and bring that exact same energy on-camera. Off-camera, when nobody's around, you can make goofy faces in the mirror, sing freely, crack jokes, totally full of confidence. But the second the camera rolls, you become a totally different person. You can tangibly feel yourself holding back. But notice that most of the time, what's suppressing you isn't the audience, it's you. It's your own conscious mind. When you drop that self-consciousness and hit a state of flow, that's when you achieve your maximum self-expression, and that is your ultimate potential. And this state of flow is the deepest form of relaxation. Very few people can actually pull it off, myself included. But for those who can, it's almost impossible for them not to succeed. Now, the stage or camera example is specific, but it's also universal, because we're all performing on the stage of our own lives. A lot of the time, we don't even realize we're suppressing ourselves. And the more you suppress yourself, refusing to release your unique personality and nature, the further away you get from the success you want. That's why, at 40 years old, I decided to pursue just being "loose," to capture that feeling of true relaxation. I get the logic, but so often I still can't help it. I still tense up and consciously criticize myself. So that leads to what I want to share next—the most important lesson I learned from Psycho-Cybernetics: and that is saying goodbye to the straw man from your past. Going back to that Marc Andreessen interview, did you notice? He said self-criticism is a guilt-driven psychological philosophy, and people trapped in it usually live in the past. Well, this book mentions the exact same thing. The author says, "Don't fight the straw man out of the past." Stop wrestling with the ghost of yesterday. A lot of the tension and self-criticism you feel right now isn't reflecting anything happening in the present; it's reacting to a threat that hasn't existed for years. It's like how the college entrance exams are long gone, but so many of us still dream about them, and most of the time, it's a nightmare. I often talk with my friends about how so many people have been stuck in survival mode for so long. Always the reactor, always on edge. But you're not in that environment anymore. You are safe now. Yet your system is still running on that old code. We don't know how to play in exploration mode. We don't know how to go back to being an actor, because we've been tense for so long we think it's the only way to live. And that scar I mentioned at the start of this video? That's my straw man. I used lies to bury it in the deepest corner. And by avoiding it, I couldn't ever say goodbye to it. It pops up from time to time to stop you from being your true self. So I'm putting it out there today, because I finally want to say goodbye to it. Your straw man might look different from mine. It might be that one time you were rejected as a kid, or a moment of public humiliation, or the fear you internalized after being punished, or the constant hint that you just weren't good enough. But whatever it is, it belongs to the past. It has no place in your present. In The Courage to Be Disliked, which we read before, there's a concept a lot of people struggle to accept: that trauma doesn't exist. It doesn't mean pain isn't real. It means your current reaction isn't being forced on you by that past event. You have a choice. This is exactly what Psycho-Cybernetics is saying too. The book makes it very clear: when faced with a fake problem, the best reaction is actually no reaction. Reacting makes you a reactor—it makes you tense. Not reacting makes you the actor—it makes you relaxed. So when we talk about learning to relax, it essentially means learning not to react to non-existent problems, to problems we've blown completely out of proportion, to emotions and environments that can hurt us. Not reacting. Capturing that relaxation, letting the straw man fall, letting your success mechanism genuinely run for you again, achieving maximum self-expression, and unlocking the next chapter of your life. This is why, at 40, I decided to stop pushing so hard, and made learning to relax a major life priority. Naval says there are three things in life: learn, earn, relax. I've spent the last 30 years doing the first two, and now it's time to start the third. Because "Good things only happen when you are relaxed."

videos.title: 大涨刚刚开始？标普500年底到多少？ | 猴哥财经 | 美股 | 投资 | 股票 | AI
videos.transcript: 哈喽，大家好，欢迎来到猴哥财经，今天是4月28日星期二，今日美股整体回调，截止收盘，道琼斯指数下跌0.05%，标普五百跌了0.49，纳斯达克跌了0.90。今日美股下跌的主要原因是华尔街日报在盘前发布了一篇文章说open AI在2025年年底和今年年初。 没有达到自己的内部收入和用户目标。那open AI作为AI的主要风向标，这样的消息出来直接导致大科技在盘前全线下跌。英伟达AMD甲骨文等等在判前均有3%到五不等的下跌幅度。但随着时间的推移，这些股票今日从最低点都有所反弹。 而这主要是因为这篇文章本质上就是旧闻加工，市场很快就看到了本质，只不过它配载了市场最有杀伤力的时间点，才导致了美股今日的下跌。一方面是最近AI相关股票的确涨了太多了，需要一个止盈出货的时机，并且也赶在了明天大科技。 海报开始的前一天，市场本来就比较敏感的时刻，所以这是一篇非常经典的定点轰炸式文稿，时机选得太刻意了。那为什么说它是旧闻？因为这并不代表整个AI市长在放缓，只不过是open AI在丢失市场份额，而实际上他们在AI应用市场上被Gemini。 和epic蚕食份额这件事从去年下半年开始就已经发酵了，没有任何人不知道，包括CEO，奥特曼在去年年底就发布过内部code red备忘录，要求提升的个性化和可靠性。而且就在上个月，open AI刚刚以巨高的估值完成了新一轮的融资。 投资人包括软银亚马逊英伟达等等，这些都是业内最聪明的钱。如果他们的业绩有任何大问题，那怎么可能还能得到如此高的估值呢？所以这篇文章没有任何新东西，他告诉我们的只是一些我们早已知道的事情，就是open AI在过去半年中把部分份额。 让给了和这和整个行业的AI资本开支节奏完全没有关系。所以我们也就看到今天的市场把这个新闻消化了以后，AI相关股票由收回了大部分的跌幅，这本身就是最好的答案。市场比头条聪明，但是还要提醒大家，这可能不是近期唯一的杂音。 在经历了最近这波AI引领的大涨以后，机构会找机会出货，而出货就需要有消息来砸盘，而这时候就要进行小题大做。那今天这则消息未能对AI股票造成太大的实质性影响，但接下来还有其他的可能性，比如说明天的美联储决议。 以及明天盘后的大科技财报，这些都可能成为机构借题发挥的头条。尤其是盘后这么多，加大科技发布业绩，想要找到坏消息并不是太难的事情。当然不发生这样的事情是最好的情况，但如果发生，我们也完全不要恐慌，因为这大概率只是机构在找理由出货。 而我们要做的就是不被这种短期的杂音和波动所影响，不要被单日的涨跌带节奏，更长远的看整体的基本面，因为基本面才是决定美股长期走势的根本原因。而从这个角度来看，我认为在经历了三月份这波回调以后，下一波大涨。 才刚刚开始，也欢迎朋友们在评论区分享。您认为美股是否还有更多的上涨空间，还是已经到顶，回调很快就要来临。那我先把今天最核心的两个判断放在前面，第一，今天的大科技估值根本没有大家以为的那么贵，实际上已经回到了AI热潮。 开始初期的水平。第二，企业盈利不只是在增长，而且增长速度和超预期幅度都是过去十年里少见的。这两件事叠在一起，得出的结论只有一个，美股这一轮真正意义上的大涨才刚刚开始，但我也要提醒大家，接下来这几天市场大概率会非常吵，非常乱。 因为连续反弹了一段时间以后，机构本来就需要找理由做短线出货，打情绪清洗筹码。今天那篇open AI的文章就是一个例子，明天盘后大科技又要集中发布财报，同一天还有美联储的利率决议，这种超级密集的窗口，本来就是机构借题发挥的时候。 财报只要有一点点不够完美，或者管理层措辞稍微保守，机构都可能先卖再说。那今天可以拿open AI的内部数字砸盘，明天就可以拿大科技财报砸盘。总之，市场永远不缺砸盘的理由，但我们一定要理智地认识到，这些都只是短期的杂音。 真正的问题不是有没有砸盘理由，而是这些理由能不能改变。AI的主线在我看来是不能的，波动不是趋势，反转，更多是机构借题发挥，借机换手。所以这期节目我不会跟大家纠结某一天盘前盘后的涨跌，我只讲两个决定，中期方向的核心问题。 第一就是为什么估值比大家以为的要便宜很多，而且盈利还在持续超预期。第二，为什么AI资本开支这个之前市场最担心的问题正在从风险故事逐渐变成变现故事？那我们先讲第一条，估值没有大家想的贵，盈利增速比大家预期的也要快。 把这两件事放在一起，我们才能够真正理解这轮行情为什么才刚刚开始。最近很多人看到指数创新高，就本能地说一句太贵了。可问题是今天的大科技真的有大家嘴里说的那么贵吗？著名的阿波罗资管的首席经济学家在上周发布了一张图，是我觉得最近最有杀伤力的。 一张图，他不只看了标普五百整体的估值，还看了标普五百信息技术板块前十大公司的估值，分别是英伟达苹果微软博通甲骨文镁光AMD思科等等。通过看这些股票的加权前瞻市盈率，也就是按照未来12个月的每股收益来计算的。 市盈率结论非常反直觉。数据显示，这批最核心的科技巨头在2024年最热的时候前瞻市盈率接近四十倍，但到现在已经押回到了二十倍左右。当然，在经历了上周的反弹以后，这些估值有小幅的增加，但也依然远低于2024年的峰值。 所以我们可以看到这些公司的估值已经回到了AI热潮初期的水平。因此我们可以非常直观地看到，现在整体大科技股的估值绝对没有到特别贵的地步，甚至是相对便宜的未知。而且这件事最重要的地方不是估值跌了，而是这些股票明明涨了很多了。 估值为什么反而回落了呢？这是因为它们的盈利长得更快，价格在涨，但分母涨得更快，所以估值就被稀释掉了。那泡沫顶部通常是什么画面？是股价继续涨，估值继续扩，但盈利却开始跟不上。可今天我们看到的不是这个画面，而是股价涨了。 盈利长得更快，结果估值反而就被压下来了。这不是泡沫顶部的特征，更像一轮主升浪的初中段。所以从估值的角度来看，现在依然是相对较低的，还有进一步扩张的可能性。如果我们把盈利的数据再放进来，这个逻辑只会更应根据facts。 最新的统计显示，标普五百指数今年第一季度的混合净利润率达到了13.4%。如果最终确认这个预期能够变成事实，那它将是faxed从2009年开始追踪这个数据以来的历史最高值。而且我们注意这并不是一次性的异常值。 上一个季度的记录是13.2，而那个记录就是在他之前那个季度刚刚创下的，已经连续多个季度再创新高。分析师对2026年全年的净利润率的预测是13.9%，而过去十年的平均水平只有11%，这也就意味着今天的企业。 每一块钱的收入能挤出来的利润比过去任何一个可比时期都要多。整个盈利能力的台阶已经被永久性地提高了一层。再看超预期的幅度，目前已经公布的第一季度财报里，84%的公司盈利超过了分析师的预期，超预期幅度平均达到了12.3%。 这两个数字在过去五年分别对应的均值是78%和7.3，这也就是说，这一季的超预期，无论是发生的比例还是幅度都是高于过去五年的均值了，所以这一系列的估值和盈利数据都指向我们没有处在很严重的泡沫，反而基本面。 有非常明显的改善迹象，甚至还在不断地加速。当然，现在大科技平均二十多倍的市盈率，当然不是特别的便宜，但也绝对不是只剩下情绪支撑的状态。他们后面站着的是订单，是积压的合同，是利润率，是现金流，是还没有体现到财报里的未来承诺金额。 那把估值和盈利这两件事放在一起，结论就变得非常清楚。今天这个市场并不是在泡沫顶部，他更像是一轮主升浪的初中段。我对年底标普五百的判断是至少八千点，也就是从当前的位置算，大概还有11%的上涨空间，这个幅度不需要估值重新从二十倍。 吹回到四十倍，只需要盈利继续增长预期继续上修。而就目前的数据来看，这两件事都在发生，而且在加速发生。所以从估值的角度来看，整体美股依然有继续走高的空间，那第二个理由也是现在市场分歧最大的地方，我们要重点讲一下。 那就是AI的变现可见度比前几个月明显提高了。之前三月份整体美股大幅回调的核心原因就是投资者认为现在AI的大幅开支最终很有可能赚不回来或者转的太慢了。但我认为现在市场对AI资本开支最强烈的那种恐惧。 正在阶段性的降温。之前市场最怕的一句话就是钱花出去了。AI到底什么时候才能把钱赚回来？那这个问题到今天的确依然没有彻底的回答完，但他已经不像前几个月前那么模糊了。最大的变化在于市场现在看到的不再只是巨头疯狂砸资本支出。 而是开始初步看到企业端的真实落地，节省下来的真实成本，以及更清晰的订单和合同和越来越强的需求可见度。那这两天有一条消息，我认为是一个里程碑的事件。就在昨天，微软宣布将Co pilot部署到知名的咨询公司埃森哲。 全球约74.3万名员工，这是迄今为止规模最大的单次企业级Co pilot部署。那埃森哲从2023年8月开始试点，从几百人的小范围测试到两万人，再到后来承诺的三十万人，到今天一次性扩展到全世界全公司74万人。 从数字上来看，这笔交易的绝对金额在大厂营收里是很小的一部分。但我认为这件事的意义远远不止是一笔订单，它代表的是一个主题的开始。全球最大的咨询公司之一，选择把AI工具全面嵌入到每一个员工的日常工作流，而不是作为可选工具。 让员工自己决定用不用那埃森哲内部的数据非常有说服力。已经使用了一段时间的二十万人员工队列里，越活越使用率达到了89%。这个数字对于任何企业软件都算极高了，更别说是一个需要额外付费的AI插件97%的员工。 表示，Co pilot帮助他们把日常重复性任务的完成速度提升了最多15倍53%，报告了显著的生产力提升。其中有一个具体案例，通过Co pilot驱动的销售分析工具，活跃使用的销售人员比未使用的同事多开发出了43%的销售机会。 这些数字都不是假设是真实的，已经跑过24万人规模的实测数据。更重要的是，这件事告诉市场的信号，当埃森哲这种全球顶级的咨询服务公司，他自己每天在帮客户做AI转型，选择把Co pilot变成全公司的标配，它传递的信号不只是我们在用。 而是这已经是可以规模化部署对生产力产生真实影响的工具了。如果他们这种在AI落地上走得最快的公司，都在今天才从三十万用户扩展到了74万，这告诉我们这个主题还在早期，未来会有越来越多的公司下类似的订单。 而这就是AI进一步大规模变现的重要信号之一。而且微软自己的数据显示，Co pilot月活用户现在是一薏仁儿Accenture这一笔就贡献了快1%，这就是我说的收入不多但主题很重要的意思，它是一个信号，一个证明AI工具已经从试用。 进入全面制度化部署的信号，而制度化部署一旦开始，就很难逆转，而且速度只会越来越快。而且不仅如此，近期有越来越多企业落地AI的证据，比如四大之一的德勤德罗，他们最新的2026年调查里显示66%。 和企业已经报告看到了生产率和效率的提升，40%的企业已经看到了成本的下降，而真正已经看到收入增长的只有20%。但我反而觉得这组数据特别健康，因为它说明AI第一阶段最真实的兑现路径本来就不是先爆发式增收。 而是先将本先省人工先省工时，先把原来需要很多人做的重复性工作自动化，所以AI帮助最先进入利润表的不一定是新收入，而是少花的钱，这才是企业AI当下最现实最可持续的起点，那这对我们打工人来说绝对是坏消息。 因为注定会有越来越多的裁员，但对企业的灵力和股价来说，绝对是一个重要的利好。另外一组数据也非常有意思，从亚特兰大联储调查近750家企业高管得到的数据来看，AI对劳动生产率的年化贡献在2025年大约是0.8个百分点。 2026年预计跳升到两个百分点以上，落到具体岗位。该调查显示，软件开发岗的生产力提升被测算为26%，新入职的客服人员效率提升达到了34%，这些数字已经开始在企业的成本报表里体现出来。 而且AI不需要100%替代掉我们现在每一个人的工作，只要能把我们的效率提升30%到五十，那这就意味着以前两个人的工作或者三个人的工作，一个人就可以做了，而这也就带来了相应比例的裁员。而且谷歌关于AI agent的研究。 进一步证明了这一点。在已经使用生成式AI的企业里，52%已经把AI agent放进了生产环境，主要集中在客服营销安全运营和技术支持这些最容易直接省钱最容易直接提效的场景。所以AIA真的来临也进一步加速了这个过程。 AI的变现落地，至少在企业端来看，路径已经变得更加清晰了。而且虽然近期市场都在批评这些云厂商资本开支实在是太高了，但我们要注意一件事，所有这些公司的管理层在最近的电话会议上都说了同一句话，就是算力是供给受限，而不是需求受限。 那他的意思就是我们想多卖，但是产能不够。所以如果大量的资本开支并不是赌未来的需求，而是变成了现在已有订单，但产能不够用，市场对它的估值框架就应该根本性的改变。对于市场来说，这个变化非常关键。AI的资本支出不再只是先把钱砸下去。 希望以后有需求，而越来越像需求合同预付款承诺已经在那里，所以现在必须扩产能。前者是一个风险故事，后者是一个能见度很高的执行故事。所以当市场开始用后者来理解AI的资本开支，那对整个AI主线的估值容忍度。 就会系统性的提升。这是我认为当前市场最被低估的一个认知转变，它还没有完全被价格反映进去，但它正在发生。所以这第二个理由简单来说就是AI不是还没开始赚钱，而是很多人还在用错误的标准看他总在等一个突然多出几百亿美元新收入的瞬间。 才愿意承认AI开始兑现。可在我们真实的世界里，AI往往先从省钱开始，先从裁掉一些岗位，少招一些人，少花一些公式开始，也就是先从费用端改善开始，然后随着部署越来越深，在慢慢长出更大的收入池。 我们今天已经能够在利润表里看到这里的第一层，第二层，也就是真正大规模的新收入。我认为还在路上，但离我们也不远了当然也正因为市场对未来两三年的变现可能性还存在很多争议，市场才会出现三月份那样的反复剧烈波动。 今天opening I这条新闻就可以被拿来砸盘。明天大科技财报一样可以先拿来卖一波。但我还是那句话，这些都只能够造成短期的震荡，但改变不了真正的方向。因为现在从基本面的角度来看，路径已经变得非常清晰。首先估值比大多数人都以为的要更加便宜。 第二就是AI的变现路径已经在变得越来越清晰，所以我认为今年年底标普五百指数至少能够涨到八千点，当然在这个过程中也会是非常坎坷的，很多杂音都会不断的给美股造成短期的震荡，那真正的大行情从来都不是在所有人。 都舒服，所有消息都完美的时候展开的。它往往就是在分期里，在波动里，在一次次是不是不行了的怀疑里，走出最有杀伤力的主绳端。所以如果今天你问我一句最简单的话，这一轮美股是不是已经涨的差不多了？我的答案依然是不是。 这轮大涨才刚刚开始。好的，以上就是本期的视频，如果您看到这里了，也一定记得给我留下一个赞，并且麻烦您在评论区留下一个手动的赞，让更多的朋友们看到这期视频。再次感谢朋友们一直以来的大力支持，我们下期节目再见。

videos.title: 五小时法则：成为富豪的习惯 | 全球顶尖成功人士遵守的成功法则
videos.transcript: 为什么我们每天努力工作 可是最后的成就却千差万别呢？ 答案其实并不在于我们在工作时间有多拼命 而是在于我们如何利用好空闲的时间 今天查理就来为大家介绍  很多成功人士都在使用的五小时法则 利用好一周中这五个小时 就能让我们在生活中 取得事半功倍的成就 简单来说 五小时法则指的是无论有多忙 每天至少将一个小时 或者每周五个小时 保留给自己用来学习 这个法则是美国开国三杰之一 本杰明·富兰克林 (Benjamin Franklin) 的时间管理原则 作为著名的物理学家和发明家 他最早提出了电荷守恒定律 他还是美国独立战争时重要的领导人之一 也是美国首位邮政总局局长 可以说 富兰克林的人生就是许多杰出人物的生命总和 那么一个人到底怎样在一生中有限的时间里 做出那么多伟大的事的呢？ 在富兰克林的相关著作中 有人挖掘出了富兰克林成年以后 一直遵循的时间管理原则 他坚持在每个工作日 周一到周五 专门空出 1 个小时进行学习 学习的内容包含各个领域 同时他还会制定个人成长目标 并会追踪和反思自己的实践效果 这样下来一周就有 5 个小时 后来人们也将这个法则称为 富兰克林的 5 小时法则 这个原则看上去极其简单 但实际上坚持下来的难度非常高 五小时法则的核心在于 给日程留白 而不在于每天 1 小时这个量化标准 但即使是保留少于一小时的空白时间 在这个信息高速运转的互联网时代 我们也很难做到 查理曾经看过一个有趣的研究 在一个对 200 多名白手起家的百万富翁 进行为期五年的研究当中发现 许多成功人士平常都不看电视 而且 令人印象深刻的是 其中有 86% 的人 都会在空闲的时间进行阅读 还有 63% 的人表示  他们在早上通勤时会听播客 股神巴菲特把 80% 的时间花在阅读和思考上 如果你看过他的每周日程表 你就会发现他甚至还有专门的理发时间 周二是理发日 这就是巴菲特 创造世界上最成功的商业记录之一的原因 他有很多时间思考 而且在整个职业生涯中 他都是这样去规划自己的时间的 为什么一家有数十万员工公司的首席执行官 会有这么多空闲时间 而我们的日程却排得满满的呢？ 其实利用好空闲时间 并不是让自己陷入忙碌 而是要创造闲暇时间 让自己有空学习 来超越生活中其他没有意义的事 并获得观察问题的新视角 把无意义的事情剔除 给生活留白 可以让我们有时间认真考虑 自己想要学习的东西 这样才能提升自己 而不是漫无目的的做事却毫无进步 既然我们一周只抽出五小时的时间学习 那就一定要学会高效输入和输出 推崇这个法则的成功人士 往往会分成三个部分来进行 首先是阅读 埃隆 马斯克 (Elon Musk) 就说过 他是通过阅读学会了如何制造火箭 并因此创立了 SpaceX 阿里巴巴创始人马云说 除了扩展你的知识 阅读可以让你有一个好的开端 这往往是你的同龄人无法获得的 与其他人相比 读者更有可能了解其他行业的战略和战术 大多数的我们作为普通人 就算没有创造商业帝国的梦想 也可以在长期的阅读习惯中获得收益 所以就算你不能保证每天阅读一个小时 或者更长时间 也不要轻易放弃 就像前面提到的 阅读一个小时不是硬性的指标 你可以选择抽出 20 到 30 分钟来开始阅读 生产力专家乔恩塞 马多克斯 (Choncé Maddox) 写道 成功人士阅读不是秘密 据说普通百万富翁每月阅读两本书或两本书以上 比如白手起家的百万富翁 马克 库班 (Mark Cuban)  和克利夫兰骑士队 (Cleveland Cavaliers) 老板丹·吉尔伯特 每天就会阅读一到三个小时 其实阅读的内容不需要难懂艰深 乔恩塞就建议 每个人可以在休息时间根据兴趣来阅读 这样你就可以吸收更多元的知识 查理自己经常会带着一本书 当我在等待会议开始前 或者坐车的时候都会阅读一两页 而不是浪费时间在智能手机上 如果你觉得很难执行 这里给大家提一个方法 就是每周给自己设定一个阅读目标 你可以每天读一章或者每月读一定数量的书 如今电子书广泛普及 无论你在哪里都可以进行几乎任何话题的阅读 比如比尔 盖茨 每年就会阅读大约 50 本不同领域的书籍 第二步是反思 反思是学习的一个关键部分 在不间断的学习过程中 一时间吸收太多信息会让你感到不知所措 这就好像你拿到了很多食材 但是没有沉下心思考要做什么菜 一堆好食材就被浪费了 所以 要从阅读中掌握新技能 反思是非常重要的 抽出时间对学习到的东西进行思考 可以让我们从中产生更多的领悟 和吸收新的观点 也能潜移默化的让我们积累想法 这样才能产生创造性的突破 德克萨斯大学的一项研究发现 精神休息和反思可以改善学习 专注于过去的经历 让我们有机会从犯过的错误中吸取教训 思考自己能否继续优化行为 进而制定更准确的目标计划 所以 谷歌有一个 20% 原则 让员工可以自由使用每周 20% 的工作时间 来放松或者做自己喜欢的事情 这样的放松不仅不会影响办公效率 反而可以激发更多的想象力和创造力 当然了 反思的时间和方式取决于你自己的安排 可以是在每天睡前将想法写在便利贴 并贴到自己专属的思考墙 又或者在一个人散步跑步时也可以进行思考 对查理来说 在每天的午餐之后 挤出 15 到 20 分钟来进行思考是比较理想的安排 因为我正从午饭后的低迷中走出来 可以通过沉思来让自己的脑细胞活跃起来 对于刚开始培养这个法则的人来说 可以从小处着手 每天就分配 5 到 10 分钟 然后慢慢的增加时间 这样你就不会在一开始时感到疲惫不想继续 同时 在思考的时候需要清楚自己想问的具体问题 注意 坚持一天只提出两到三个问题 并深入的思考这些问题的答案 例如  如果今天你参加了一个会议 那么在自己的反思时间时 你可以问自己 参加这个会议关键收获是什么？ 和我如何将这些知识应用到我的业务中？ 最后一个部分是快速实验 本杰明·富兰克林 (Benjamin Franklin) 和托马斯·爱迪生 (Thomas Edison) 正是因为他们的实验 而成为领先的发明家和思想家 实验如此有用 是因为它让我们看到事实而不是假设 也就是说 实验会向你展示什么是有效的 你可以从错误中学习 并从别人那里获得反馈 如果你想在生活中取得进步 实验是必不可少的 每周留出一些时间来测试新理论或想法 不管它们有多疯狂 世界上一些最成功的产品 比如马斯克的地下隧道就是实验的结果 创新永远不会来自一遍又一遍地做同样的事情 即使你的实验失败了 你也会学到宝贵的经验 所以 把阅读到的知识和理论实践于生活中 可以让你感觉到阅读的实际好处 也可以让你更加坚持阅读 其实实验没那么耗时间 大多数在你没意识到的时候 你已经把阅读的内容应用或实践于日常生活中 比如 在阅读了一本关于协作和团队合作的书后 你可以从事新的志愿者工作来运用这些知识 或者在阅读大量投资新闻和财报后 对有信心的公司进行长期定投 这些都是可以学习和验证自己想法的机会 人们很容易把工作和学习混为一谈 这就是为什么你最终会感到停滞不前 或许有人会觉得 我每周工作 40 小时 我肯定会有进步的 但事实并非如此 重复的工作会让你容易形成惰性思维 当你专注于每天一样的日常问题时 你没有给自己更多的时间和空间去发展和成长 对于很多人来说 他们通过完成的任务量来衡量自己的工作表现 因此 他们在一天中急速前进 却不愿意放慢脚步 提高自己的改进率 而五小时法则反其道而行之 它首先关注的是刻意的学习 而不是每天去工作的时候 希望自己能顺便从中学到什么东西 如果你一直专注于当前的工作 而不是长期的自我提升 你将永远看不到太多的发展 这就是为什么提出五小时法则的富兰克林认为 即使在那一天他会完成较少的事情 他也情愿花上至少一个小时进行学习 因为从长远来看 这可谓是他对自己的时间所做的最好投资 为了方便大家理解 查理给大家举个简单的例子 很多销售人员在打电话之前都不会做任何研究 他们直接拿起电话就打 打完了就继续拨打下一个电话 一天下来他们可以打很多通销售电话 甚至几百个  工作效率看似达到了 但他们从中收获了什么呢？ 答案可能是没有 不具有主动学习思维的人 不能从这些重复的电话中学习到什么东西 而一个具有学习关注点的人就会去想明白 在打电话时要练习的技巧 比如要怎么样才能让客户更信任我 不会想要立马挂电话？ 这样的人会在过程中进行练习 并反思从中取得的收获 如果他还想真正的进行更高水平的学习 他可以在打电话时邀请一位同事去听 并在之后请那位同事提出真实的反馈 当你具有主动学习的观念 并采取了这样的生活方式 这就意味着每打出一个销售电话 你就会在这方面有所改进 把焦点放在学习可以改掉我们的自动化行为 这样才能保持进步而非停滞不前 我们生活中的每件事情 都是一个用来提升自己的机会 有意识的学习才能让我们进步 而不是止步不前 通过聚焦于学习的生活方式 我们可以在长期完成更多的事情 每周给自己五个小时的学习时间 可能很难得到立竿见影的回报 但从长远来看 你会看到巨大的进步 给自己设定具体的学习目标 把时间花在成为最好的自己上 给自己时间去实现它们 这些经历慢慢积累 可以帮助你在通往成功的路上走得很好 曾有人总结大佬们的工作方式 关键不在于如何工作 而在于如何休息 无论有意无意 更多的企业家和业界精英们 在验证着这个 5 小时法则 希望它能在繁忙的日常中 帮你学到能够改变你人生的知识与技能 那么 你已经准备好开始执行五小时法则了吗？

videos.title: 公開我買過的全部股票：四年來究竟賠了多少錢？
videos.transcript: Hey! You're right According to a lot of comments I've been a scammer for 4 years. 這4年間呢 我總是以「害」我的觀眾輸錢為快樂 甚至看我穿著Grab的背心 就很多的指控說我收了Grab的錢 所以幫他們打廣告 哪一支股票下跌了 那鐵定就是我收了他的錢在幫他打廣告 So Now that I'm leaving, You guys deserve to know the TRUTH 所以今天 我會把過去四年我在自己的頻道 影片、直播、別的節目所有地方 我曾經講過的所有股票 一次列出來給你看 當時我說我買進的價格&現在的價格 Everything you need to know and this should put the conversation to sleep for good 因為過去4年 在投資上面我做了很多功課 付出了很多時間 做了很多正確的決定 買了很多好股票 當然也犯了很多錯誤 買了不少的爛股票 那今天這一份名單呢 或是說這份資料呢 就可以讓你自己判斷 Maybe I'm just a scammer lol Alright so without further ado guys Let's get the BGM rolling for one last time! 所以透過這份資料 應該很明確可以看到 過去4年我確實買入了不止一兩支 害我虧錢的爛股票 但你也可以很清楚看到 不管你相不相信這份資料的真實性 不管你要怎麼調整這份資料 讓他看起來更貼近現實 那事實就是 我們的投資組合過去4年 是毫無疑問擊敗大盤的 That is the "big picture" 這也是為什麼我常跟大家說 You've got to focus on the big picture 因為長期投資他的魅力就是 You are allowed to make mistakes and you will make mistakes and you will keep making mistakes 我這邊說的"you"可能是你，也可以是我 我以後也會繼續選到爛的股票啊 And so what? 所以這種東西 你可以說他是"Negativity Bias" 心理學叫做Negativity Bias 就是今天如果你發生了九件好事 但你發生了一件壞事 你就會覺得今天是個"Bad Day" and guess what? 如果你今天發生了九件壞事和一件好事呢？ It's an even worse day!!! 所以人們會傾向於 或者說人們就天生會去放大負面的東西 這就是我剛剛講的Negativity Bias 那如果長期投資跟主動選股 是你選擇的道路的話 Then you gotta deal with Negativity Bias 因為不可能你投資的這個生涯呢 都不犯任何一個錯誤 Nobody's perfect and you DON'T have to be perfect! 你如果覺得這個頻道 不能帶給你任何價值的話 Then so be it I'm sure there are other places that fits you more 但我不希望的是 你因為「負面偏誤」而懷疑自己 你可以不喜歡這個頻道的風格 這個頻道的投資組合 或者這個頻道分析的方式 但是如果你的投資組合跟我類似 Mostly you are picking winners 但你因為一兩個losers 讓你自己過意不去的話 那你其實沒有做好身為投資者 最重要的一個功課：Focus on the big picture  As an investor, you make mistakes; you learn and then you move on you don't stuck! You move on you focus on the big picture So yep that's pretty much all I got for you  I got one more video for you though:) 下一支影片會跟大家分享 我離開YouTube一陣子的原因是什麼 好啦如果你把影片看到這邊的話 我就想跟你說 Thank you for the support I appreciate you ; I love you and I hope you have a great day and I will see you in the next video

我當然我如果只是希望說 我是一個influencer 但是我是不是要做到一個大到一個 就比本業還要強大 的一個influencer 我可以控制的 所以像我就可能在YouTube 我就做一個非常 算一個微型的 就是維持它在一個很小的一個 一個量級那podcast 維持在我一個比較沒有壓力的 就是公司他也不會真的來關注我 我如果做到太大 公司隨時在檢查我的那個言論 我覺得做得也不高興不高興 如果來賓來分享可以 那怎麼去要什麼題目要怎麼回答 這個我們就不做 雖然很多人會做 而且網路上非常多人做 你如果看那種被concern的 基本上都是做這種東西的 對那我們就會避開 所以其實在就是 其實各種場合我們都會講說 哪些東西是絕不做的 那目前看來四年來 也沒有就沒什麼問題 對就是就是就是避開 我覺得這就是一個對就是一個盡量 對盡量避開公司不喜歡的 但是我如果做 什麼Apple Microsoft 我就用力做了 因為我不是 Apple Microsoft的 我就用力做就可以做那些題目這樣子 沒錯 對對對對對 OK好非常感謝抹布 今天來跟大家分享這麼多 謝謝謝謝 有趣的東西 我覺得一定一定非常有幫助啊 然後大家如果有興趣的話 也可以去關注一下 你們的podcast 科技工作講對科技工作講 對對對 OK好那影片最後跟大家分享一下 我跟波爾的現代系統設計實戰營 已經開課了 大家如果感興趣的話呢 現在是最後的機會可以參與 那我們設計這個實戰營的初衷呢 就是希望在這個AI時代 大家可以在面試的時候脫穎而出 並且拿到你心目中外商的offer 同樣都是工作 為什麼不選擇一家更好的公司 不只是薪水會有很明顯的提升 我跟波爾都認為 這會給你帶來的是跳躍階級的進步 在現在這個時代對於一家公司來說 最重要的一個實力之一 就是你能不能夠設計一個 能夠scale up到上百萬 甚至上千萬且穩定的系統 而且系統設計也是 我們認為接下來五年 軟體工程師最重要最基本的一個要求 那我們的實戰營呢 也會在線上live 帶著大家一起做一些 更hands on的project 也會用到不少現在AI的技術 這些也都是 蠻不錯的side project 你可以放在你的履歷上面 那如果你不想錯過 最後報名的機會的話呢 可以在影片底下找到連結 或者是說到我們的官網 buildmoat.org
